context.predecessor: omega-runner-1 TARGETARCH-conditional COPY (~2.3MB/arch saved); omega-runner-2 perl removal (~50MB layer saved)
context.host: darwin/arm64; cross-strip via homebrew aarch64-linux-musl-strip + x86_64-linux-musl-strip

pre_strip.marker_arm64: ELF ... statically linked, with debug_info, not stripped

strip_applied.where: tool/build_runner_image.hexa added module-level fn strip_elf(elf_path, strip_bin) called for all four ELFs after presence checks, before docker buildx invocation
strip_applied.soft_fail: if cross-strip binary not in PATH, prints SKIP and continues (no fail-stop); if file already stripped, no-op
edits: /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/tool/build_runner_image.hexa: added strip_elf helper + four call sites + comment block

distroless_attempt.candidate_base: gcr.io/distroless/cc-debian12 (~22MB virtual; debian12-bookworm derived so libc/libssl ABI compatible with our debian:12-slim build deps)
blockers_found: 16 nexus bridges (oeis/gw/arxiv/simbad/wikipedia/openalex/uniprot etc) exec curl directly via shell; any libcurl/libssl ABI drift between builder stage and distroless cc-debian12 causes silent TLS-handshake failures; needs full bridge_health smoke before cutover

tini binary must be COPY'd from debian builder stage; PPID==1 watchdog in cmd_run() depends on tini-as-PID-1 contract (per Dockerfile comment block re: 2026-04-25 SIGKILL incident); needs a dedicated PPID-orphan smoke test inside distroless image

distroless lacks /bin/sh, so any RUN steps in the final stage become impossible — all install/chmod must move to builder stage, then COPY --from=builder; current Dockerfile has a RUN that does install -m 0755 + sanity --version probe that would need refactor
wget transitive (libssl3, libgnutls30, libidn2, libpsl5, libpcre2, libuuid1) adds ~4MB; some bridges use wget rather than curl — verify and possibly drop wget if no consumer; saves another ~4MB
no shell means no in-container debug (docker exec hexa-runner /bin/sh fails); diagnosis cycle requires a debug-tagged variant kept in parallel
distroless_attempt.recommended_cutover: no this cycle; YES as separate cycle omega-runner-4 with explicit bridge_health smoke + PPID watchdog test gates; deferred
distroless_attempt.risk_level: medium-high (bridge fan-out + PID 1 contract); reversible (keep debian:12-slim Dockerfile as fallback)

size_delta_actual.note: actual virtual delta (1MB) is LOWER than the raw 760KB-on-disk savings would predict because docker layer overhead amortizes; compact delta (0.3MB) is small because debug_info compresses well in the layer tarball; HOWEVER both arches benefit identically when the multi-arch manifest is pushed (so registry storage saves ~1.5MB across both arches)

evidence: docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 hexa-runner:latest help => HEXA banner + USAGE block displayed
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 -v /tmp/smoke.hexa:/work/smoke.hexa:ro hexa-runner:latest run /work/smoke.hexa => 'hello from stripped runner' (interp + cli round-trip works post-strip)

gates: all 16 nexus bridges PASS bridge_health under distroless image
PPID-orphan watchdog test (force-kill parent shell, verify tini reaps watcher subshell without SIGKILLing workload prematurely)
Refactor Dockerfile final stage RUN -> builder stage RUN + COPY --from=builder
Keep debian:12-slim Dockerfile in parallel as docker/runner/Dockerfile.fallback for emergency revert

next_cycles_seeded.rationale: if no nexus bridge actually invokes wget (curl handles all HTTPS), drop wget from apt layer to save ~4MB (libgnutls30 + libidn2 + libpsl5 + libpcre2 + wget binary itself); requires grep across nexus bridges for 'wget' invocations

next_cycles_seeded.axis: alpine + musl base prototype (research-grade, parallel to distroless)
next_cycles_seeded.rationale: alpine:3.19 is ~7MB virtual; with musl-built ELFs already in hand, and apk add ca-certificates tini curl wget at ~3MB, total image could approach 12-15MB virtual
next_cycles_seeded.blockers: musl libc DNS resolver differs from glibc (no NSS); some bridges using getaddrinfo with .local mDNS may break; full bridge smoke required; defer

rollback.elf_revert: rebuild from source: hexa run tool/build_hexa_cli_linux.hexa --target=arm64 (and x86_64) + tool/build_interp_linux.hexa --target=arm64 (and x86_64); the rebuild produces unstripped ELFs by default
rollback.image_revert: docker tag hexa-runner:94cd2b41 hexa-runner:latest (the prior 166MB image is still in local engine until image rm)
rollback.blast_radius: low — strip is purely a debug-symbol removal; runtime semantics unchanged; smoke test PASS confirms; reversible by re-running cli/interp builds
rollback.production_safety: no production push; --push flag NOT used; only local --load to host engine

constraints_honored.hexa_only_session: code edits limited to docker/Dockerfile-adjacent infra + tool/ build script (raw-73 admissibility per session note: hexa-only allowed; Dockerfile changes are infra — but no Dockerfile change was needed this cycle, only the build script)
constraints_honored.no_linux_elf_rebuild_from_source: honored — strip applied to existing build/ artifacts in place
constraints_honored.stop_when: JSON written + strip applied (production image rebuild verified PASS)

ledger_observation.actual_vs_target: target was '100MB+ savings beyond ω-runner-1 4MB'; actual production image savings 1MB virtual / 0.3MB compact — well below target; distroless pathway projected at 74MB but blocked on bridge smoke gating, deferred to omega-runner-4
ledger_observation.honesty: the 100MB target was achievable ONLY via base-image swap (distroless or alpine), not via strip alone; strip was the safe immediate lever (low blast, easy revert, smoke-verified) and is now baked into the build pipeline so future rebuilds stay stripped without manual intervention; the bigger lever (distroless) is correctly deferred behind bridge-smoke + PID-1 contract gates rather than rushed
